adminPanel.title: Admin Panel
adminPanel.description: Access the full admin dashboard to manage users, view analytics and predictions.

scan.title: Skin Image Analysis
scan.subtitle: Upload an image or use your live webcam for instant AI skin analysis.
scan.cameraHint: Please allow camera access in your browser settings and try again.
scan.tip: Position the skin area inside the blue frame. Ensure good lighting for better accuracy.
scan.loadingSubtitle: CNN model is processing your image. Please wait.
scan.fusionTitle: Symptom-based decision fusion is under development
scan.fusionDesc: Currently, prediction is based solely on the uploaded image. In a future update, these symptoms will be combined with image analysis to improve diagnostic accuracy.
scan.awaitingSubtitle: Upload image files or capture photos on the left. The AI analysis will appear here.
scan.tip1: Ensure good, bright lighting on the spot.
scan.tip2: Upload multiple angles or distances of the same skin area.
scan.tip3: Position spots clearly centered in the frame.

prediction.title: AI Prediction Result
prediction.heatmapPending: Heatmap will appear here once Flask backend is connected
prediction.disclaimer: This is an AI prediction for awareness only. Please consult a certified dermatologist for proper diagnosis.

diseaseInfo.title: Disease Awareness
diseaseInfo.subtitle: Learn about the 10 skin conditions detected by DermaLens. Click any card to see full details.
diseaseInfo.highWarning: High risk — consult a dermatologist immediately if you notice these symptoms.
diseaseInfo.mediumWarning: Medium risk — monitor regularly and see a doctor if symptoms worsen.
diseaseInfo.disclaimer: Images shown are medical reference illustrations. Always consult a certified dermatologist for proper diagnosis.

acne.name: Acne
acne.description: A common skin condition where hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells, causing pimples, blackheads and whiteheads.
acne.visualDesc: Inflamed red bumps or whiteheads commonly on face, chest and back

athleteFoot.name: Athlete's Foot
athleteFoot.description: A contagious fungal infection that usually begins between the toes, common in people whose feet get sweaty inside tight footwear.
athleteFoot.visualDesc: Scaly, cracked skin between the toes or on the soles, often with redness and itching
causes: Walking barefoot in public showers or pools

cellulitis.name: Cellulitis
cellulitis.description: A bacterial infection of the deeper layers of skin that can spread quickly and requires prompt medical treatment.
cellulitis.visualDesc: Red, swollen, warm patch of skin with an indistinct border that can enlarge rapidly
causes: Bacterial entry through a break in the skin

eczema.name: Eczema
eczema.description: A chronic inflammatory skin condition causing itchy, red and cracked skin. Often linked to allergies or asthma.
eczema.visualDesc: Dry, scaly and inflamed skin — commonly on hands, elbows and behind knees

impetigo.name: Impetigo
impetigo.description: A highly contagious bacterial skin infection common in children, causing sores that ooze and form a honey-colored crust.
impetigo.visualDesc: Red sores, often around the nose and mouth, that rupture and form a yellowish-brown crust
lookFor: Red sores near nose or mouth

ringworm.name: Ringworm
ringworm.description: A fungal infection of the skin causing a ring-shaped red rash. Despite the name, it is not caused by a worm.
ringworm.visualDesc: Circular red ring with clearer skin in the middle — itchy and scaly

rosacea.name: Rosacea
rosacea.description: A chronic skin condition causing redness and visible blood vessels in the face, sometimes with acne-like bumps.
rosacea.visualDesc: Persistent redness across nose and cheeks — may have small pus-filled bumps

shingles.name: Shingles
shingles.description: A painful rash caused by reactivation of the chickenpox virus, usually appearing as a band on one side of the body.
shingles.visualDesc: Cluster of fluid-filled blisters in a band or strip, typically on one side of the torso or face

urticariaHives.name: Urticaria (Hives)
urticariaHives.description: Raised, itchy welts on the skin that appear suddenly, often as an allergic reaction, and can shift location within hours.
urticariaHives.visualDesc: Red or skin-colored welts that appear suddenly and may change shape or location
causes: Physical triggers like heat or pressure
lookFor: Welts that change shape or location

vitiligo.name: Vitiligo
vitiligo.description: A condition where skin loses its pigment cells, resulting in white patches on the skin.
vitiligo.visualDesc: Smooth white patches on skin — clearly defined edges where pigment is lost
lookFor: Hair in patch may be white

precautions.title: Precautions & Skin Care
precautions.subtitle: Follow these guidelines to maintain healthy skin and prevent skin diseases.
precautions.warningBody: If you notice any rapidly changing skin lesion, immediately consult a certified dermatologist. Early detection saves lives.

checks.title: Regular Skin Checks
tips: Look for new or changing moles
Use the ABCDE rule for moles

hydration.title: Skin Hydration
tips: Drink at least 8 glasses of water
Avoid hot showers — use warm water

doctor.title: When to See a Doctor
tips: Mole changes in size or color
Any skin change that worries you
